ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region to which the country belongs */,
  "subregion" TEXT /* Subregion to which the country belongs */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Name of the capital city */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency used in the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language(s) of the country */,
  "government_type" TEXT /* Type of government (e.g., Republic, Monarchy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_data" (
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "yearly_change" REAL /* Yearly change in population */,
  "net_change" INTEGER /* Net change in population */,
  "density" REAL /* Population density per square kilometer */,
  "age_distribution" TEXT /* Age distribution data */,
  "urban_population" INTEGER /* Number of people living in urban areas */,
  "rural_population" INTEGER /* Number of people living in rural areas */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "gender_ratio" REAL /* Ratio of males to females */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the population data (e.g., UN, World Bank) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_statistics" (
  "health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Life expectancy in years */,
  "meat_consumption" REAL /* Meat consumption per capita */,
  "suicide_rate" REAL /* Suicide rate per 100,000 people */,
  "infant_mortality_rate" REAL /* Infant mortality rate per 1,000 live births */,
  "maternal_mortality_rate" REAL /* Maternal mortality rate per 100,000 live births */,
  "healthcare_expenditure" REAL /* Healthcare expenditure as a percentage of GDP */,
  "physicians_per_1000" REAL /* Number of physicians per 1,000 people */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the health data (e.g., WHO, CDC) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_statistics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fertility_rates" (
  "fertility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fertility data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "fertility_rate" REAL /* Fertility rate per woman */,
  "age_distribution_fertility" TEXT /* Age distribution of fertility rates */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the fertility data (e.g., UN, World Bank) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fertility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_fertility_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "urbanization_data" (
  "urbanization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each urbanization data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "urban_population_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of urban population */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "urban_population_count" INTEGER /* Number of people living in urban areas */,
  "rural_population_count" INTEGER /* Number of people living in rural areas */,
  "urban_growth_rate" REAL /* Annual growth rate of the urban population */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the urbanization data (e.g., UN, World Bank) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("urbanization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_urbanization_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "land_area" (
  "land_area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each land area data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "land_area" INTEGER /* Land area in square kilometers */,
  "water_area" INTEGER /* Area of water bodies in the country */,
  "total_area" INTEGER /* Total area (land + water) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the land area data (e.g., UN, World Bank) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("land_area_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_land_area_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "migrants" (
  "migrant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each migrant data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "migrants_net" INTEGER /* Net migration rate */,
  "in_migration" INTEGER /* Number of people immigrating to the country */,
  "out_migration" INTEGER /* Number of people emigrating from the country */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the migrant data (e.g., UN, IOM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("migrant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_migrants_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position or role within the department */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "dataset_version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
